Military Apr 7, 2026

Chilean KC-135 Tanker Refuels USAF F-35As on their Way to FIDAE 2026 Expo

For the first time, a Chilean KC-135 refueled two U.S. F-35s that will take part in FIDAE 2026, marking the traditionally strong strategic ties between Chile and the U.S. A Chilean Air Force (Fuerza Aérea de Chile – FACh) KC-135E tanker aircraft refueled two U.S. Air Force F-35A Lightning IIs for...

Chile's first air-to-air refueling of U.S. F-35s demonstrates deepening military interoperability and signals Chile's strategic importance in Latin American defense partnerships.

See The Aviationist →

Military Apr 7, 2026

The A-10C Can Now Use Probe and Drogue Refueling

A new Probe Refueling Adapter, developed in response to an urgent combatant command requirement, allows the A-10 to significantly expand its refueling options as it is currently dependent on the KC-135. Few days after a photo circulated on social media, the Air National Guard Air Force Reserve...

A-10s can now access probe-and-drogue refueling from allied tankers, reducing operational dependence on limited KC-135 assets and improving sortie sustainability in contested environments.

See The Aviationist →

Military Apr 7, 2026

5 Aircraft That Define Modern Air Combat

The warplanes at the top of the food chain.

Understanding modern air combat aircraft capabilities is essential for commercial aviation professionals navigating airspace shared with military operations and regulatory frameworks.

See Simple Flying →

PAI
Safety Apr 7, 2026

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Effective SMS relies on psychological safety that encourages frontline workers to report hazards without fear, directly reducing unreported safety risks in operations.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

NASA’s Artemis II Crew Beams Official Moon Flyby Photos to Earth

The first flyby images of the Moon captured by NASA’s Artemis II astronauts during their historic test flight reveal some regions no human has seen, including a rare in-space solar eclipse. Released Tuesday, astronauts captured the images April 6 during the mission’s seven-hour flyby of the lunar...

Artemis II's lunar flyby documented previously unobserved lunar regions and captured a rare in-space solar eclipse, providing crucial visual data for refining human Moon landing procedures.

See NASA →

Business Aviation Apr 7, 2026

Learjet Overruns Runway at Teterboro

No injuries reported after landing incident at New Jersey airport.

Runway overruns at high-traffic business airports like Teterboro highlight recurring challenges with aircraft braking performance and weather conditions during peak operations.

See AVweb →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Air Canada Unveils 2 Surprising New Long-Haul Airbus A321XLR Routes For 2026

Air Canada's bold move to launch launch flights to this destination is highly intriguing, but will it pay off?

Air Canada's A321XLR deployment signals carriers are aggressively chasing point-to-point long-haul markets previously reserved for widebody aircraft, challenging hub-and-spoke economics.

See Simple Flying →

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Safety Risk Management's transition from theoretical analysis to operational controls requires organizations to systematically document residual risk acceptance decisions under regulatory requirements.

Singapore Airlines operates the largest fleet of this popular aircraft.

Singapore Airlines' A350 dominance reflects long-haul carrier strategy to maximize fuel efficiency and passenger capacity on premium transcontinental routes.

Hungary taps Northrop Grumman for first national geostationary communications satellite

Hungary’s defense and space tech contractor 4iG also announced deals with L3Harris and Apex for defense systems and satellite manufacturing...

Hungary's satellite partnership with Northrop Grumman signals Central European investment in independent space infrastructure amid broader NATO modernization trends.

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

Teledyne FLIR OEM, part of Teledyne Technologies Incorporated (NYSE:TDY), today announced an upgrade to Prism™ SKR (pronounced...

Enhanced automatic target recognition in thermal imaging systems could improve mission effectiveness and reduce operator workload for defense and surveillance platforms.

Safety Apr 7, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 Departure requires heightened pilot attention due to specific procedural complexities that differ from standard RNO departures on parallel runways.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Safety Apr 7, 2026

Southwest Airlines Limiting Passengers To 1 Portable Charger Onboard To Reduce Fire Risk

Southwest Airlines will limit passengers to one power bank from April 20, tightening safety rules amid rising lithium battery fire risks onboard.

Southwest's restriction signals industry recognition that cumulative lithium battery density poses systemic cabin fire risk beyond current FAA regulations.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

Earthset

The Artemis II crew captured this view of Earth setting on April 6, 2026, as they flew around the Moon. As the astronauts flew over the Moon’s far side, the crew photographed and described terrain features including impact craters, ancient lava flows, and surface cracks and ridges formed as the...

Lunar orbital missions now provide unprecedented high-resolution Earth and lunar surface imagery that advances both space exploration documentation and Earth observation capabilities for aerospace applications.

See NASA →

General Aviation Apr 7, 2026

Picture of the Day: April 7, 2026

A J-3 Cub spreads its wings over the desert.

The timeless J-3 Cub continues to embody the spirit of accessible, affordable flying that defined general aviation's golden era and remains relevant to modern recreational pilots.

Safety Apr 7, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway exit design complexity at major hubs creates systematic incursion risks that require pilot awareness beyond standard procedures.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Military Apr 7, 2026

Charleston Airshow Moves Amid Ongoing Conflict

Joint Base Charleston says 2026 show was changed to an off-base due to operational requirements tied to current global events.

Military airshows are increasingly relocating off-base due to heightened operational security concerns, signaling how geopolitical tensions are reshaping public aviation event logistics.

See AVweb →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Delta Air Lines Becomes 3rd US Carrier To Hike Checked Bag Fees As Fuel Prices Soar

Delta Air Lines joins United and JetBlue in hiking checked baggage fees, following a sharp rise in global jet fuel prices.

Rising fuel surcharges are now driving ancillary fee increases across major carriers, signaling airlines expect sustained elevated fuel costs rather than temporary market volatility.

See Simple Flying →

Military Apr 7, 2026

Kraus Hamdani Aerospace Awarded $270 Million AFCENT Contract to Accelerate Deployment of K1000ULE

Kraus Hamdani Aerospace (KHA) has been awarded a $270 Million single-source IDIQ rapid deployment contract...

AFCENT's single-source contract signals accelerated military adoption of the K1000ULE platform, bypassing traditional competitive procurement to prioritize rapid operational deployment.

See sUAS News →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational efficiency improvements across airlines, airports, and flight planning can address capacity constraints while simultaneously reducing aviation's environmental footprint without waiting for breakthrough technologies.

See MDPI →

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

Experience the Rollout of SLS Hardware for Artemis III

Digital creators and social media users are invited to register to attend the rollout of the third SLS (Space Launch System) core stage as it is prepped to head from NASA’s Michoud Assembly Facility to NASA’s Kennedy Space Center. Are you passionate about social media and communications? Do you...

NASA is leveraging digital creators and social media influencers as force multipliers for public engagement around Artemis III infrastructure milestones, recognizing that grassroots content distribution drives broader aerospace industry awareness.

See NASA →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Why The Boeing 777-300ER Is Preferred By Passengers

The 777-300ER is one of the most common widebody airliners currently in the skies.

The 777-300ER's passenger preference stems from its superior cabin comfort features and efficiency, making it a competitive advantage for airlines operating long-haul routes.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Intellian advances ESA for Panasonic, reveals further aero aspirations

Having made a name for itself in the maritime industry as a leading provider of flat panel antennas, including in support of Eutelsat OneWeb Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite-powered connectivity, Intellian recently burst onto the aviation scene when it was selected by Panasonic Avionics to produce a...

Intellian's antenna technology transition from maritime to aviation through Panasonic signals growing competition in in-flight connectivity provision as LEO satellite operators expand aerospace market reach.

See Runway Girl Network →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

5 Aircraft With The Most Innovative Cabin Designs

In the heated, competitive world of aircraft cabin design, five aircraft and operators stand out from the competition.

Cabin innovation differentiates operators in a commoditized market, directly influencing passenger experience and airline competitiveness beyond aircraft performance specs.

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

NASA to Host Media Call with Artemis II Crew on Way Home from Moon

Media will have an opportunity at 9:45 p.m. EDT on Wednesday, April 8, to speak with NASA’s Artemis II crew members as they continue their journey toward Earth during their historic mission around the Moon. The 20-minute virtual news conference will take place with NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman,...

The in-flight media engagement demonstrates NASA's commitment to public communication during lunar missions, establishing a precedent for astronaut accessibility during deep-space operations.

See NASA →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Alaska Airlines Makes History: Boeing 737 MAX Transatlantic Flights Launch Next Month

It will become the carrier's new longest narrowbody service ever, but there's a twist...

Alaska Airlines' transatlantic 737 MAX service will test whether single-aisle aircraft can profitably compete with widebodies on long-haul routes, potentially reshaping capacity planning across US carriers.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Apr 7, 2026

Pilots’ Group Warns of Conflict-Zone Strain

New position paper says repeated uneventful flights should not reduce scrutiny.

Pilot fatigue from routine conflict-zone operations can create dangerous complacency, requiring airlines to maintain consistent safety protocols regardless of flight history.

See AVweb →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Press Release: FlightPath3D map gets extra smart with Luci Live launch

The inflight map is now an intelligent, interactive information engine, thanks to the launch of Luci Live. Developed by FlightPath3D, the premier provider of immersive airline passenger maps, and unveiled at Aircraft Interiors Expo in Hamburg this month, Luci Live adds a customizable, AI travel...

FlightPath3D's AI-powered Luci Live transforms passive inflight maps into revenue-generating interactive platforms by personalizing passenger engagement and enabling targeted airline services.

See Runway Girl Network →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Press Release: WTCE reveals trends reshaping APAC onboard hospitality

World Travel Catering & Onboard Services Expo (WTCE) has released its newest report, exploring the cultural, culinary and commercial forces transforming inflight hospitality across Asia’s rapidly expanding aviation landscape. With Asia-Pacific airlines transporting 390.5 million international...

Asia-Pacific's explosive passenger growth demands airlines rethink catering strategies beyond traditional models to compete on experience differentiation.

See Runway Girl Network →

Regulatory Apr 7, 2026

FAA Budget Request Seeks 2,300 Controller Trainees

Agency ties proposed hiring increase to staffing, training and modernization needs.

The FAA's trainee expansion directly addresses the operational bottleneck created by retirement waves and NAS modernization demands, signaling confidence in near-term traffic recovery.

13 Minutes Of Terror: The Real Reason This TAP Flight Turned Back

Portugal's aviation authorities have shared further details on the incident that occurred two months ago.

Unrevealed mechanical or operational issues forced an immediate return, suggesting TAP's incident involved more serious safety concerns than initially disclosed to the public.

Drones Apr 7, 2026

Major PX4 drone software vulnerability raises hijacking concerns

A widely used piece of drone software just got a serious cybersecurity wake-up call, and if you operate drones in the US, it’s something you’ll want to pay attention to. more…...

PX4's vulnerability exposes thousands of commercial and recreational drones to potential mid-flight hijacking, making cybersecurity patching critical for operators and manufacturers nationwide.

US Army launches Anduril Altius 700 drone from Apache for the first time

The US Army has for the first time launched a drone from an AH-64E Apache attack helicopter, firing…...

Apache helicopters can now deploy swarm drones mid-flight, multiplying battlefield reconnaissance and strike capabilities without requiring additional launch platforms.

City alleges missing fees, absent records and diverted tuition receipts at municipal GA airport.
